LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT PUBMATIC, INC.'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE UNOPPOSED MOTION TO STAY IS GRANTED. THE COURT'S USUAL PRACTICE IS TO FIX AN END DATE FOR STAYS. THE CASE IS STAYED FOR ALL PURPOSES THROUGH JULY 13, 2026. IN THE EVENT THE MOTION TO DISMISS IN THE FEDERAL ACTION IS RESOLVED EARLIER, THE PARTIES MAY STIPULATE OR MOVE TO LIFT THE STAY EARLIER. ORDER SUBMITTED IS UNDER REVIEW. MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Jan-30-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PUBMATIC, INC.'s MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The motion for pro hac vice is granted as to Mitchell J. Clough.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the modified tentative ruling and must deliver it to Dept. 301.

Motion And Unopposed Motion To Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Jan-05-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT PUBMATIC, INC.'S UNOPPOSED MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS

No appearances. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: The unopposed motion to stay is granted. The court's usual practice is to fix an end date for stays. The case is stayed for all purposes through July 13, 2026. In the event the motion to dismiss in the federal action is resolved earlier, the parties may stipulate or move to lift the stay earlier. Order submitted is under review. Judge: Christine Van Aken, Clerk: E. Larnauti, Not Reported
